items.q: What does Spearman's rank correlation coefficient measure?
items.correct: The strength and direction of a relationship between two ranked variables. Values range from -1 (perfect negative) through 0 (no correlation) to +1 (perfect positive).
items.misconception: A Spearman value close to 1 proves that one variable causes the other.
items.random_wrong: Spearman's rank measures the average rank of all the data points.
items.why: Spearman shows correlation strength, not causation. Significance testing is also needed to check the result is unlikely by chance — students report rs values without context or significance level.

items.q: What weather does an anticyclone (high pressure system) bring?
items.correct: Descending air warms and dries, suppressing cloud formation — so anticyclones bring clear, settled weather: hot in summer, cold and frosty in winter.
items.misconception: High pressure brings stormy weather because the air pushes down hard.
items.random_wrong: Anticyclones bring heavy rainfall because they trap moisture.
items.why: Students conflate 'high pressure' with 'strong weather'. In fact, sinking air evaporates clouds — depressions (low pressure with rising air) bring storms, not anticyclones.

items.q: Why is ocean acidification a separate threat from ocean warming?
items.correct: Acidification occurs because oceans absorb atmospheric CO2, forming carbonic acid and lowering pH — this dissolves calcium carbonate shells and corals, independent of any warming.
items.misconception: Ocean acidification is just another name for ocean warming.
items.random_wrong: Ocean acidification is caused by acid rain falling on the sea.
items.why: These are two distinct CO2-driven changes: warming affects heat-sensitive species, acidification affects calcifiers (corals, molluscs, plankton). Even with zero warming, acidification would still threaten marine food webs.
The answer examiners want is: Foreign Direct Investment. “Federal Domestic Industry” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which term describes adapting global products to local tastes?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Glocalisation. “Outsourcing” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Approximate number of TNC affiliates worldwide (thousands)?
items.why: The real figure is 500. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
